summary_one_line: An ICC profile header (esp. device-class field at 0x0c) whose parsed tag table demands a seek offset exceeding the small data buffer.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A small, structurally-valid ICC profile header where parsing reaches MemorySeek with an offset greater than ResData->Size. The winner seeds carry the device-class signature 'link' (6c 69 6e 6b) at offset 0x0c and a profile flags byte 0x01 at 0x2d, and are tiny (523-538 bytes) so a parsed tag/element offset readily exceeds the buffer Size, taking the TRUE 'Too few data; probably corrupted profile' branch.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are 523-538 bytes with device class 'link' (DeviceLink) at 0x0c-0x0f and profile flags byte 01 at 0x2d; with such a small data buffer a tag/element offset parsed from the header overruns ResData->Size, so MemorySeek takes the blocked TRUE side (W shows T=7).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are large (9321-25244 bytes) with device class 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at 0x0c-0x0f and flags byte 00 at 0x2d; the large buffer comfortably contains every parsed offset, so MemorySeek always falls through to the FALSE branch (L shows T=0 F=69) and never resolves the blocker.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. The cmplog CMP buffer logs the magic/header comparand bytes (e.g. the device-class and signature fields the parser compares against ICC constants), and I2SRandReplace splices those exact bytes into the input at the matching offsets, letting vpc assemble a well-formed small-buffer header that drives parsing to an out-of-bounds seek. Plain value_profile has only Hamming-distance comparison feedback and no substitution mutator, so it cannot reliably place the header bytes that funnel into the over-size seek.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked TRUE side of MemorySeek fires when the seek offset exceeds the buffer Size.
The winner resolves the blocker 9/10 trials while the loser is blocked 10/10.
The loser never resolves the branch.
Winner seeds carry the device-class bytes 'link' at offset 0x0c-0x0f whereas loser seeds carry 'mntr'.
Winner seeds are small (523 bytes) while loser seeds are large (tens of KB).
A loser-blocking seed is 25244 bytes, far larger than the winner's buffer.
Winner seeds have profile flags byte 01 at 0x2d while loser seeds have 00.
In MemorySeek the winner takes the TRUE side (T=7) but the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 174  T=7 F=9  T=0 F=69  if (offset > ResData ->Size) {

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog uniquely adds the I2SRandReplace substitution mutator.
Plain value_profile has only CMP_MAP feedback and lacks I2SRandReplace.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2135 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0551798f555a68d1`; the ancestor chain (8 levels) contains 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 7, the ParentInfo-only signature characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog/vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves MemorySeek's TRUE side at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog despite the header device-class/signature fields being CMP-gated, showing the substitution mutator confers no advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the device-class 'link' header byte to a seek offset exceeding Size is inferred from seed size and source structure, not directly observed in the byte dataflow into the MemorySeek offset argument.
